description_long: This bollard is a tall, rectangular column constructed from dark, weathered stone blocks, giving it a robust and historic appearance. The surface features a mix of smooth and rough textures, with some areas showing more pronounced brickwork and others appearing more eroded or worn. The top of the bollard is capped with a slightly overhanging, flat stone slab, adding a sense of solidity and permanence. The base is broad and square, providing stability and a decorative touch with a stepped design. Subtle reddish-brown hues and dark patches suggest exposure to the elements or possible fire damage, enhancing its aged look. There are no visible connectors, chains, or reflective markings, and the object appears to be a fixed, immovable barrier.
description: A tall, rectangular stone bollard with a weathered, blocky texture, capped by a flat stone slab and set on a broad, stepped base. Its dark, aged appearance and solid construction evoke a sense of permanence and durability.
description_view_0: This view shows a mostly smooth stone face with a vertical strip of rough, protruding brickwork on the left side.
description_view_1: This side features a smoother surface with less pronounced brickwork, and subtle discoloration suggesting weathering.
description_view_2: The front displays a uniform, blocky brick pattern across the entire face, with the most regular and defined stonework.
description_view_3: This view reveals a mix of rough and smooth textures, with a patchwork of bricks and some areas appearing more eroded.
